zh.lifeSummary: Richard Barker是Richard Barker的兒子，於1505年10月出生在英國素福爾可納易藍德。他於1539年之前與Alice Kinge在英國素福爾可納易藍德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1561年7月27日在他的家鄉享年55歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可納易藍德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Barker是Richard Barker的儿子，于1505年10月出生在英国素福爾可納易藍德。他于1539年之前与Alice Kinge在英国素福爾可納易藍德结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1561年7月27日在他的家乡享年55岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可納易藍德。

nameMeanings.name: Barker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a tanner of leather, from Middle English barkere ‘tanner’, tree bark having been used as the tanning agent.

English: occupational name for a shepherd, from Middle English berker, bercher (Old French berchier, bercher, berkier, berker, Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’, genitive berbicis). With the change of -ar- to -er- in Middle English, this became indistinguishable from the preceding name (see 1 above).
Americanized form of German   Berger   or   Barger  .

variants.name: Berger
meanings: German, Dutch, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived in the mountains or hills (see   Berg  ). The surname of German origin is also found in many other European countries, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Russia, Poland, Czechia, Hungary, and Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic topographic names or surnames. As a Jewish name it is mainly artificial. Compare   Bargar  ,   Barger  ,   Barker  ,   Barrier  , and   Barriger  .
French: occupational name from Old French bergier ‘shepherd’ (from Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’). It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin. Compare Shepard .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farms so named with the plural of   Berg   ‘mountain’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: Richard Barker was born in October 1505, in Nayland, Suffolk, England as the son of Richard Barker. He married Alice Kinge before 1539, in Nayland, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 27 July 1561, in his hometown, at the age of 55, and was buried in Nayland, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Barker est né en octobre 1505, à Nayland, Suffolk, Angleterre, fils de Richard Barker. Il a épousé Alice Kinge avant 1539, à Nayland, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 27 juillet 1561, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Nayland, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Barker wurde im Oktober 1505 in Nayland, Suffolk, England, als Sohn von Richard Barker geboren. Er hat vor 1539 in Nayland, Suffolk, England Alice Kinge geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 27. Juli 1561 im Alter von 55 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Nayland, Suffolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Barker è nato il nel ottobre 1505 nel Nayland, Suffolk, England come figlio di Richard Barker. Lui sposato Alice Kinge prima del 1539, nel Nayland, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 27 luglio 1561, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolto a Nayland, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Barkerは1505年10月にイギリススフォルクナイランドでRichard Barkerの息子として生まれた。1539年以前にイギリススフォルクナイランドでAlice Kingeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1561年7月27日に故郷で55歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクナイランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Barker(은)는 1505년 10월 영국 수폴크 나이랜드에서 Richard Barker의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1539년 이전 영국 수폴크 나이랜드에서 Alice Kinge(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1561년 7월 27일 그의 고향에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 나이랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Barker nasceu em outubro de 1505, em Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra filho de Richard Barker. Ele casou-se com Alice Kinge antes 1539, em Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 27 de julho de 1561, em sua cidade natal, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Barker родился октябрь 1505 г. в местности неиланд, суффолк, Англия. Его отца звали Richard Barker. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Kinge перед 1539 г. в местности неиланд, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер 27 июля 1561 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности неиланд, суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Richard Barker nació en octubre de 1505, en Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra como hijo de Richard Barker. Se casó con Alice Kinge antes de 1539, en Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 27 de julio de 1561, en su pueblo, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultado en Nayland, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
